name: @ironflyer/ide
description: Ironflyer IDE — custom-branded Eclipse Theia browser application, served per-workspace and embedded as an iframe inside the studio.
//comment-type: Theia generates CommonJS output; unlike the other clients (studio/marketing) this package intentionally does NOT set "type": "module".

theia.//target-comment: browser target = served over HTTP and iframe-embeddable; no Electron shell.

preferences.//workbench-comment: Suppress the Getting Started / welcome editor on startup (Theia honors workbench.startupEditor like VS Code).
preferences.//trust-comment: Disable Workspace Trust entirely: the workspace is an isolated per-user sandbox provisioned by Ironflyer, so the 'Do you trust the authors of this folder?' modal is pure friction and blocks the editor until dismissed.
preferences.//theme-comment: Default to the custom dark theme contributed by @ironflyer/theia-branding.
preferences.editor.fontFamily: 'Geist Mono', 'JetBrains Mono', 'SFMono-Regular', Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace
preferences.//telemetry-comment: Theia has no remote telemetry by default; these keep notification clutter down.
preferences.//chrome-comment: Menu bar is hidden for the embedded iframe look via branding.css (owned by the branding ext). 'window.menuBarVisibility' and 'workbench.activityBar.location' are NOT set here: they are not reliably honored by Theia 1.60 and the activity bar/sidebar must stay visible for file navigation.

config.//comment: startupTimeout -1 disables the watchdog so large workspace mounts don't get killed during first scan.
